div.div:
request_materialization(pathname)
1. socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM | SOCK_CLOEXEC)
2. connect $MXL_INTENT_SOCK (default /run/mxl/agent.sock)
3. write "{\"path\":\"
abspath
\"}\n"
4. read until \n (buffer ≤ 1024 B)
5. close
6. return 0 if "\"ok\":true" substring is found, else -1

return whatever it gives

div.div:
Why the path test is so narrow
The shim is loaded into every process via LD_PRELOAD,
including libc init, dlopen lookups, dynamic linker probes,
and every /etc/* config read. is_flow_def_path() is a pure
string check (no syscall) so the fast path stays cheap.
Only paths shaped like:
/
any
/<flowID>.mxl-flow/flow_def.json
ever reach the UDS round-trip.

switch.text: Why the path test is so narrow...
